title: Copy, Replace, Rename, Delete, Batch, and Concurrency · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent team receives different tenants, file sizes, media types, regions, compliance duties, traffic, failures, and cost limits and transfers copy, replace, rename, delete, batch, and concurrency into a new conditional object-mutation protocol. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of conditional object-mutation protocol with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Copy or replace with explicit source version, conditional evidence, metadata directive, encryption, checksum, tags, and destination validation.
Implement rename as version-aware copy, validation, metadata update, reference switch, and safe source deletion with recoverable intermediate states.
Use validators, version IDs, idempotency records, or application locks to detect stale concurrent mutation and expose 409 or 412 meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Copy Replace Metadata
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 3120 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to model a non-atomic version-aware copy, validate, and delete rename protocol; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Copy or replace with explicit source version, conditional evidence, metadata directive, encryption, checksum, tags, and destination validation.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Copy Replace Metadata.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for copy or replace with explicit source version, conditional evidence, metadata directive, encryption, checksum, tags, and destination validation. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Copy Replace Metadata, keep Copy Replace Metadata connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 3120 during a regional dependency incident. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. CopyObject automatically preserves every source property and authorization rule. That claim does not survive the commit to the mission model evidence boundary.
For commit to the mission model, run one convenient Copy Replace Metadata happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Audit the initial evidence packet · Lab Batch Partial Failure
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go file-delivery team handles case 6392 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to model a non-atomic version-aware copy, validate, and delete rename protocol; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Chunk batch operations, correlate per-object outcomes, retry only eligible items, preserve authorization, and report partial success accessibly.

Inspect the Batch Partial Failure trace for this lab case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Batch Partial Failure
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: storage-go-batch-partial-failure-1l203c" that uses case 7880 to model a non-atomic version-aware copy, validate, and delete rename protocol. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import AWS SDK packages, open sockets, contact S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, execute host commands, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5 compile, vet, fuzz, race, leak, controlled AWS, load, fault, security, cost, restore, and production gates.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, run one convenient Batch Partial Failure happy path, omit ownership, error, changed input, goroutine stop, and full-toolchain checks, then accept plausible output.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Batch Partial Failure condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.
